perturbation.original_text: Each of the South Padre Island Area OCS Blocks 1113,1059,1060,1073 and 1133 and the North Padre Island Area OCS Block 998 shall be defined as a "Project Field.'' For purposes ofthis Agreement, Project Completion shall consist oftwo components and be defined as: A) each Project Field in the Development Project having all planned in-field and flow pipelines, caissons, platforms and production facilities for all the wells in such Project Field, for which Guaranty has loaned funds, installed and operationalsuch that the production from all such wells can be transported to a readily available sales point for naturalgas.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the South Padre Island Area OCS Blocks 1113,1059,1060,1073 and 1133 and the North Padre Island Area OCS Block 998 shall be defined as a "Project Field.'' For purposes of this Agreement, Project Completion shall consist of two components and be defined as: A) each Project Field in the Development Project having substantially completed in-field and flow pipelines, caissons, platforms and production facilities for all the wells in such Project Field.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines Project Completion as having 'all planned' infrastructure operational. This change alters the definition to 'substantially completed' infrastructure. This introduces ambiguity, especially because later in the same section it outlines specifics that seemingly require 'all planned' infrastructure (e.g., transportation to a sales point).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the South Padre Island Area OCS Blocks 1113,1059,1060,1073 and 1133 and the North Padre Island Area OCS Block 998 shall be defined as a "Project Field.'' For purposes of this Agreement, Project Completion shall consist of two components and be defined as: A) each Project Field in the Development Project having substantially completed in-field and flow pipelines, caissons, platforms and production facilities for all the wells in such Project Field, for which Guaranty has loaned funds, installed and operationalsuch that the production from all such wells can be transported to a readily available sales point for naturalgas. In addition, for each Project Field, Project Completion will include, but not limited to: a) obtaining required permits, easements and governmental approvals; b) executing necessary" construction contract(s); c) completing tests considered usual and customary" and required to be conducted with results tn accordance with those necessary" to permit operations; d) ensuring that each Project Field is free and clear of all bens other than those in favor of Guaranty and Permitted Liens under the Credit Agreement and: e) causing all costs of the Development Project to be paid when due; and, B) the 12-inch loop pipeline fromNorth Padre Island Area OCS Block 996 to the pipeline owned by the Williams Companies Inc. having been constructed and installed in accordance with the plans and specifications in the construction contracts). As consideration for Guaranty entering into the Credit Agreement to provide such loans for the Development Project and to ensure Project Completion. Prime absolutely and unconditionally warrants to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of all costs that exzeed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement, including interest, for Project Completion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Project Completion' from requiring 'all planned' infrastructure to 'substantially completed' infrastructure. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text, appearing later, outlines specifics (like transporting production to a sales point) that imply a requirement of 'all planned' infrastructure being operational. The ambiguity introduced by 'substantially completed' creates uncertainty about the obligations for achieving Project Completion, thus introducing an in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Prime will, during the term of the Credit Agreement, maintain liquidity consisting of unused revolver availability" under the Credit Agreement dated December!, 2002, as amended, with Prime et aland Guaranty, and/or unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of $25,000,000.
perturbation.changed_text: Prime will, during the term of the Credit Agreement, maintain liquidity consisting of unused revolver availability under the Credit Agreement dated December!, 2002, as amended, with Prime et aland Guaranty, and/or other assets as determined by Prime at its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a clear definition of liquidity as unused revolver availability and/or unrestricted cash. The changed text broadens this definition to 'other assets as determined by Prime,' creating an inconsistency because the $25,000,000 requirement and dollar-for-dollar reduction based on shareholder advances is contingent on that cash liquidity, so is now ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licuieitv Maintenance Prime will, during the term of the Credit Agreement, maintain liquidity consisting of unused revolver availability" under the Credit Agreement dated December!, 2002, as amended, with Prime et aland Guaranty, and/or other assets as determined by Prime at its sole discretion. This required liquidity" win reduce dollar-for-dollar with any additional shareholder advance s and increase dollar-for-dollar to a maximum of $21,000,000 with any repayment of shareholder advances. To the extent that shareholder repayment has occurred, Prime agrees to fund additional shareholder loans equal to the amount repayed by the shareholder, as needed to ensure Project Clomp let ion This Agreement shall remain in force until each component ofProject Completion is satisfied. Once a component is satisfied, Prime's absolute and unconditional warranty to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of ad costs that esceed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement for that conponent, including interest, wid ejpire.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity into the definition of 'liquidity' by adding 'other assets as determined by Prime at its sole discretion'. This contradicts the subsequent clause in the 'Contradicted Text' that specifies the 'required liquidity' will reduce dollar-for-dollar with shareholder advances. The lack of a defined valuation of 'other assets' creates a direct inconsistency because the $25,000,000 requirement is contingent on the previous definition and the shareholder advance reduction is now open to interpretation. As per the Perturbation Explanation, this creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the valuation of liquidity and renders the shareholder advance reduction clause ambiguous and likely unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Prime understands that a breach ofobligations under this Agreement would result in an Event ofDefault under the Credit Agreement with Offshore that would permit Guaranty to pursue its available remedies under the Credit Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Prime understands that a material breach of obligations under this Agreement, as reasonably determined by Prime, would result in an Event of Default under the Credit Agreement with Offshore that would permit Guaranty to pursue its available remedies under the Credit Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that *any* breach would result in default. Now, a *material breach* as determined by Prime is required. This provides Prime with discretionary control over what constitutes a breach significant enough to trigger default. This conflicts with the unconditional warranty language in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As consideration for Guaranty entering into the Credit Agreement to provide such loans for the Development Project and to ensure Project Completion. Prime absolutely and unconditionally warrants to Guaranty to fund the payment to Offshore of all costs that exzeed the available commitments under the Credit Agreement, including interest, for Project Completion. In the event Offshore is in Default under Section 7.1(f), (g), (h) and/or (i) ofthe Credit Agreement, then Prime absolutely and unconditionally warrants to Guaranty" the assumption of all costs for Project Completion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that *any* breach results in default, triggering Prime's unconditional warranty. The modified text limits this to a 'material breach' as determined by Prime, giving Prime discretion. This directly contradicts the 'absolutely and unconditionally warrants' language in the contradicted section, as Prime can now argue that a breach is not material and thus avoid the warranty obligation, creating a clear in-text contradiction based on obligation.
